{{Short description="Contraction of an object's length along the direction of motion at relativistic speeds}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Length contraction
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Special relativity]], [[Lorentz factor]], [[Lorentz transformation]], [[Time dilation]], [[Spacetime]]
| formula = <math>L = L_0/\gamma</math>
}}

'''Length contraction''' (or '''Lorentz contraction''' or '''Lorentz–FitzGerald contraction''') is the phenomenon that a moving object's [[length]] is measured to be shorter than its proper length (the length measured in the object's rest frame). Along with [[time dilation]], it is one of the primary consequences of [[special relativity]].

:<math>L = \frac{L_0}{\gamma} = L_0\sqrt{1 - \frac{v^2}{c^2}}</math>

where:
* <math>L_0</math> is the [[proper length]] (length in the object's rest frame)
* <math>L</math> is the contracted length observed from a frame moving at velocity <math>v</math>
* <math>\gamma</math> is the [[Lorentz factor]]

== Key points ==
* Contraction occurs only along the direction of motion
* Dimensions perpendicular to motion are unaffected
* The effect is symmetric: each observer measures the other's lengths as contracted
* At everyday speeds (<math>v \ll c</math>), the effect is negligible (<math>\gamma \approx 1</math>)
* As <math>v \to c</math>, <math>L \to 0</math> — extreme contraction

== History ==
The idea was first proposed by [[George Francis FitzGerald|George FitzGerald]] (1889) and independently by [[Hendrik Lorentz]] (1892) to explain the null result of the [[Michelson–Morley experiment]]. They proposed that the interferometer arm contracted along the direction of motion through the [[luminiferous aether]].

[[Albert Einstein]] later derived length contraction from the principles of special relativity (1905) without reference to an aether.

== Experimental evidence ==
* [[Michelson–Morley experiment]] — null result consistent with length contraction
* Muon lifetime — atmospheric muons reach Earth's surface due to length contraction of the atmosphere in the muon's frame
* [[Particle accelerator]] experiments — relativistic particles exhibit predicted behaviour

== Proper length vs. observed length ==
| <math>v/c</math> | <math>\gamma</math> | <math>L/L_0</math> |
| 0 || 1.000 || 1.000 |
| 0.1 || 1.005 || 0.995 |
| 0.866 || 2.000 || 0.500 |
| 0.99 || 7.09 || 0.141 |
| 0.9999 || 70.7 || 0.0141 |

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Lorentz factor]]
* [[Lorentz transformation]]
* [[Special relativity]]
* [[Time dilation]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Special relativity]]
[[Category:Length]]
